subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the emotions and message in the letter to soldiers
Identify key expressions of gratitude and hope
Develop basic reading comprehension and vocabulary
Learn the structure of simple letters
Reflect on personal responsibility and civic values

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to the Chapter
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on why we write letters
Teacher explains importance of expressing gratitude
student_activities: Identify feelings expressed in the paragraph
List words that show gratitude or encouragement

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Letter
student_activities: Summarize the main idea in 2–3 sentences

lesson_plan.title: Soldiers’ Qualities
student_activities: Make a list of soldier qualities
Discuss in pairs which qualities are most important

lesson_plan.title: Reflection on Bravery
teaching_strategy: Teacher explains the importance of bravery
student_activities: Write a few sentences describing one brave act mentioned

lesson_plan.title: Letter Writing Practice
teaching_strategy: Discuss how to include feelings of respect and hope
student_activities: Write a short letter to a soldier expressing gratitude
Include at least two qualities or actions you admire

lesson_plan.title: Vocabulary and Revision
student_activities: Learn and use new vocabulary in sentences

teacher_tip: Encourage students to discuss the feelings of soldiers and their families. Ask them to write letters connecting personal reflections to the text.
